title: Buying Committee Mapping - The Deal Killers

questions.question: What is 'Single Threading' and why is it dangerous?

options.text: Using only one email thread. It makes inbox zero hard.
Relying on only one contact at a prospect account. If they leave or get blocked, your deal dies.

questions.explanation: B2B deals involve 6-10 stakeholders. Relying on one person is a single point of failure.

questions.question: Does the 'Compliance/Security' person (Technical Evaluator) usually care about your ROI?

options.text: No. They care about risk reduction, compliance, and not breaking their infrastructure.
They care about your logo color.

questions.explanation: Don't pitch ROI to Legal/IT. Pitch safety, compliance, and ease of integration. Their job is to protect the company.

questions.question: What is the primary role of a 'Champion'?

options.text: To sell your product internally when you are not in the room.

questions.explanation: Your Champion is your inside partner. They want your solution to succeed so they look good. You must arm them with materials.

questions.question: What is the 'Top-Down + Bottom-Up' (Pincer) strategy?

options.text: Emailing the VP (Strategy) and the Manager (Pain) simultaneously to create internal alignment.
Clicking mouse buttons up and down.
Emailing everyone in the company the same message.

questions.explanation: Engaging both levels creates a conversation between them. 'Do we need this?' (VP) -> 'Yes!' (Manager).

questions.question: If a prospect 'Ghosts' you after a good demo, what is the most likely cause?

options.text: A hidden blocker (who you didn't identify) killed the deal behind closed doors.

questions.explanation: Ghosting is rarely accidental. It usually means an unspoken 'No' from a stakeholder you failed to engage.

questions.question: Map out a typical buying committee for your product. Who are the key stakeholders (Champion, Economic Buyer, Technical Evaluator, etc.) and what does each care about?
questions.promptForAI: Evaluate the buying committee mapping. Check for: (1) key roles identified, (2) understanding of each role priorities, (3) navigation strategy. Suggest how to address each stakeholder.
